jokes.text: Parkour! - Michael-- - Parkour! Parkour! - Extreme! Parkour! - Parkour!
jokes.setup: Middle-aged office workers attempting trendy physical activity
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic but clearly incompetent execution
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Michael's desperate attempt to seem cool and current. The repetitive shouting builds absurdity.

jokes.text: So technically they are doing parkour As long as point 'a' is delusn and point 'b' is the hospital.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the 'technical' definition of parkour
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by implying they'll get injured
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim joke - dry, observational, perfectly timed. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Truck to refrigerators to dumpster, 360 spin onto the pallets, backflip gainer Into the trash can.
jokes.setup: Planning an elaborate parkour sequence
jokes.punchline: Ending in the trash can reveals the futility/danger
jokes.explanation: Andy's overconfident planning that's clearly beyond his abilities. The escalation to impossible moves is funny.

jokes.text: Parkour.
jokes.setup: After presumably failed attempt at elaborate parkour
jokes.punchline: Single defeated word after chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation. Single word carries all the disappointment and reality check.

jokes.text: Go put them all in random order. Then come back here For your next assignment concerning their order.
jokes.setup: Giving intern a seemingly productive task
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's pointless busywork that creates more work
jokes.explanation: Great Michael logic - sounds important but is completely meaningless. Perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: He didn't do anything sexual. He just made far, far too many monica lewinsky jokes.
jokes.setup: Reference to Michael's 'Monica Lewinsky incident'
jokes.punchline: Subverts sexual expectation - just inappropriate jokes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that's perfectly Michael. The 'far, far too many' emphasizes the absurd excess.

jokes.text: If they get married before i do, i'm gonna kill myself.
jokes.setup: Discussing potential intern romance
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to being romantically behind
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - dramatic overreaction about relationships. Dark but consistent with character.

jokes.text: This place is like spaniard fly.
jokes.setup: Commenting on office romance
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'Spanish fly' as 'Spaniard fly'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism. Shows his attempt at sophistication while revealing ignorance.

jokes.text: Wait, there's not gonna be a shotgun? - Nope. - No shotgun? 'cause that changes everything.
jokes.setup: Discussion of wedding arrangements
jokes.punchline: Jim's mock disappointment about lack of forced marriage
jokes.explanation: Good Jim humor - playing with the shotgun wedding trope in a self-aware way.

jokes.text: Free at last. Free at last.
jokes.setup: Learning there's no shotgun at wedding
jokes.punchline: Mock Martin Luther King Jr. reference about 'escaping' marriage
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that plays on the irony - he actually wants to marry Pam. Good economy.

jokes.text: I guess i have a face you can trust. I think it's because of my low cheekbones.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why people tell him things
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous pseudo-scientific explanation about cheekbones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - taking credit while making up absurd physiological reasons.

jokes.text: I should have known. Poopball?
jokes.setup: Michael recounting being tricked into fake sport
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name should have been obvious
jokes.explanation: Great cringe humor - Michael's gullibility revealed through absurd fake sport name.

jokes.text: She said the third wheel is what makes it a tricycle.
jokes.setup: Michael sharing his mom's wisdom about being a third wheel
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point with literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - completely literal interpretation that misses emotional point. Perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Stanley was with his wife. I waved at him but he didn't wave back.
jokes.setup: Andy clarifying Stanley wasn't on his date
jokes.punchline: Random detail about Stanley ignoring his wave
jokes.explanation: Andy's oblivious social observation adds to the awkwardness. Minor but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Stanley is way past the middle of his life, Especially considering his height to weight ratio.
jokes.setup: Correcting that Stanley is having midlife crisis
jokes.punchline: Brutal calculation about his life expectancy
jokes.explanation: Dark but typical Michael - inappropriate personal observations delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: If that was his sister, then what they were doing was totally illegal.
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Stanley was with his sister
jokes.punchline: Implying incest instead of just public displays of affection
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate escalation. Dark humor but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It is wonderful to be the center of attention.
jokes.setup: Michael describing the joy of having Stanley affair gossip
jokes.punchline: Admitting he just wants attention regardless of consequences
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael narcissism - revealing his true motivations for spreading gossip.

jokes.text: Hey, stanley, where you going, big guy? Up to no good?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be subtle about Stanley's affair
jokes.punchline: Completely obvious fishing attempt
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete lack of subtlety. Cringeworthy but predictable.

jokes.text: Pays $5 million. Nudity required.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to get Stanley to reveal affair through fake movie casting
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate escalation to nudity
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperation leads to inappropriate territory. Shock value over cleverness.

jokes.text: Time to stop being polite and get real.
jokes.setup: Michael deciding to confront Stanley directly
jokes.punchline: Reality TV reference for office gossip situation
jokes.explanation: Michael treating office drama like reality TV. Good character reference usage.

jokes.text: Racial profiling gossip.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he made mistake about Stanley
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate attempt to deflect with racism claim
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate deflection. Cringey but relies on discomfort over cleverness.

jokes.text: We used to go on these long walks on the treadmill.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his relationship with his nurse
jokes.punchline: Contradiction - walks on treadmill aren't long distance
jokes.explanation: Subtle absurdity - romantic walks that go nowhere. Good character detail.

jokes.text: You can't put words back in your mouth. What you can do is spread false gossip
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he can't undo gossip
jokes.punchline: Solution is to spread more gossip to confuse everything
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - solving problem by making it bigger. Perfect character stupidity with internal logic.

jokes.text: It's like the end of spartacus. I have seen that movie half a dozen times And i still don't know who the real spartacus is.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing situation to Spartacus
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point of the famous scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to sound cultured while revealing complete misunderstanding. Brilliant writing that's become iconic.

jokes.text: She's an 'anorexitec.'
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say anorexic
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing as 'anorexitec'
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael malapropism. Shows his attempt at medical terminology while revealing ignorance.

jokes.text: Do you like it as much as you like men's butts? 'Cause you're gay.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Andy about tea preference
jokes.punchline: Absurd connection between tea and homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate and illogical connection. Relies more on discomfort than cleverness.

jokes.text: This is not the first time rumors about me being gay have come up. Twice before, actually. Just a weird coincidence.
jokes.setup: Andy addressing gay rumors
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's coincidence while admitting it's happened three times
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character work - his obliviousness to obvious pattern. Self-aware but missing awareness.

jokes.text: But if he was persistent, i might give in a little bit To just really see what it felt like.
jokes.setup: Andy's hypothetical scenario with Brad Pitt
jokes.punchline: Detailed consideration of giving in to male advance
jokes.explanation: Andy's confused sexuality exploration. Uncomfortable but character-driven cringe comedy.

jokes.text: What exactly is my responsibility here? To comfort insecure heterosexual men? That can't possibly fall to me.
jokes.setup: Oscar dealing with Andy's sexuality crisis
jokes.punchline: Rejecting responsibility for straight men's insecurities
jokes.explanation: Excellent Oscar joke - intelligent, observational, perfect character voice. Great commentary on the situation.

jokes.text: And you're gonna regret that when you find yourself between a moose and her cubs at night.
jokes.setup: Intern rejecting Dwight's offer to call anytime
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific survival scenario threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - specific survival scenario delivered with complete seriousness. Absurd but character-perfect.

jokes.text: One of you will make a great mother.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving career predictions to male interns
jokes.punchline: Unexpected motherhood prediction for men
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight logic failure - treating motherhood as career option for men. Absurd but delivered seriously.

jokes.text: You know, a baby conceived out of wedlock is still a bastard.
jokes.setup: Jim being congratulated on pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Angela's harsh judgment on unmarried pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela - judgmental and inappropriate but completely in character. Dark but expected.

jokes.text: If i can't scuba, then what's this all been about? What am i working toward?
jokes.setup: Creed worried about asthma affecting scuba diving
jokes.punchline: Revealing scuba diving is his entire life purpose
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect Creed moment - existential crisis over scuba diving. Brilliant absurdist character comedy that became iconic.

jokes.text: You think i'm the voice of the taco bell dog?
jokes.setup: Oscar hearing rumors about himself
jokes.punchline: Completely random and impossible rumor
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd rumor that makes no sense. Good escalation of Michael's false gossip strategy.

jokes.text: You told people that i use store-Bought manure When i showed you where my manure comes from.
jokes.setup: Dwight confronting Michael about false rumor
jokes.punchline: Pride in homemade manure source
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - taking pride in manure authenticity. Absurd but completely character-consistent.

jokes.text: I spread the rumor that somebody here was a j. Crew model and i was referring to myself.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining J.Crew model rumor
jokes.punchline: Admitting he made up flattering rumor about himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael narcissism - spreading positive rumor about himself. Character-perfect vanity.

jokes.text: Because a lot of people think that models, Even though they're very attractive are somewhat vapid. It set our industry back quite a bit.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why being called a model is bad
jokes.punchline: Talking about modeling as 'our industry' and its setbacks
jokes.explanation: Michael identifying with modeling industry despite not being a model. Great delusional character work.

jokes.text: Her breasts were a tiny bit bigger. At first i thought, oh, she has a new bra with padding. But then i thought, pam doesn't need padding.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining how he knew Pam was pregnant
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate detailed observation about Pam's body
jokes.explanation: Typical Kevin inappropriate observation. Cringey but predictable character behavior.

jokes.text: That is the inside of your vagina.
jokes.setup: Looking at ultrasound photo
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of what ultrasound shows
jokes.explanation: Michael's medical ignorance delivered with confidence. Inappropriate but character-consistent.

jokes.text: We should have realized that you are an equal part of this.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about being left out of pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Pam's sarcastic agreement that Michael is part of their pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pam sarcasm - dry delivery that cuts through Michael's narcissism. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Hey, what up, cynthia?
jokes.setup: Michael answering phone call from Stanley's wife Teri
jokes.punchline: Calling her by the wrong name (Stanley's mistress's name)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael disaster - calling wife by mistress's name. Great callback and cringe moment.

jokes.text: I thought it was 500. Oh. I guess i didn't learn anything.
jokes.setup: Intern stating what they learned about paper reams
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing they were wrong and learned nothing
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment about futility of internship. Simple but effective self-deprecation.

jokes.text: I learned that if you look even the tiniest bit like jet li, Michael will call you jet li all summer.
jokes.setup: Intern sharing what they learned during internship
jokes.punchline: Michael's racial insensitivity disguised as friendliness
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Michael's inappropriate but well-meaning behavior. Sharp character insight.
